answers.question: Should __all__ detection be static-only or also runtime?
answers.answer: Static-only for now. Only literal list/tuple of string literals at top-level is supported (R4). Dynamic __all__ construction is deferred.

answers.question: How should star-import behave for submodules vs packages?
answers.answer: Focus on module-level __all__. Package __init__.py __all__ controls what from pkg import * exposes — same mechanism.

answers.question: Should __all__ affect from X import name (explicit imports) or X.name access?
answers.answer: No (R5). __all__ only restricts star-imports. Explicit imports and attribute access are unaffected.

answers.question: What happens when __all__ references a name that does not exist in the module?
answers.answer: Raise AttributeError at import time (R3/AC3): 'module <name> has no attribute <missing_name>'.
